title: Case Study B: Long-Range Conflict with EIS + VC Synergy
description: A 9-node environment explored via two divergent paths from Entrance Hall. A directional error at step 5 silently propagates 15 steps before a structural conflict is detected.

steps.observation: You walk south from the entrance hall into a long corridor with stone walls.
You walk east from the entrance hall into a spacious office with large windows.
Leaving the office, you head down the hallway to the meeting room.
steps.note: ERROR: LLM maps "down the hallway" to south. Ground truth is east. No conflict yet — silent error.

steps.observation: From the meeting room, you go south into a break room with a coffee machine.
steps.note: Correct edge but inherits the positional shift.

steps.observation: Going further south, you reach a storage area stacked with boxes.
Heading east from storage, you enter a dusty archive filled with old records.
Going south from the archive, you descend into a secure vault.
steps.note: Five downstream nodes have now inherited the shift.

steps.observation: From the corridor, you walk east into a well-equipped laboratory with beakers and microscopes.
steps.note: CONFLICT DETECTED at step 20. Lab would be at (1,1), but Meeting Room is already there. 15-step temporal gap!

repair.vc_observation: You walk east from the entrance hall into a spacious office with large windows.
repair.analysis: Clear directional cue "walk east" matches the labeled direction.

repair.vc_observation: Leaving the office, you head down the hallway to the meeting room.
repair.analysis: Text says "head down the hallway"  which was mapped to south, but "down the hallway" is spatially ambiguous. Given the office faces east, "down the hallway" likely means east.
repair.action: REPAIR: change direction from south to east
